description: Help develop, build, flash, and debug ESP32/ESP8266 firmware using Espressif ESP-IDF on Linux/WSL. Use when the user asks about ESP-IDF project setup, configuring targets, menuconfig, building, flashing via esptool/idf.py, serial monitor, partition tables, sdkconfig, troubleshooting build/flash/monitor errors, or automating common idf.py workflows from the command line.

procedure.description: [Goal] Provide a repeatable, command-line-first workflow for ESP-IDF development on Linux/WSL: configure вҶ’ build вҶ’ flash вҶ’ monitor вҶ’ debug/troubleshoot.

[Quick start (typical loop)] ### Method 1: Activate ESP-IDF first (Recommended)
```bash
# 1) Source the ESP-IDF environment (once per terminal session)
cd /path/to/esp-idf
. ./export.sh

# 1.1) Enable ccache to speed up compilation (recommended)
export IDF_CCACHE_ENABLE=1

# 2) Go to your project and build
cd /path/to/your/project
idf.py set-target <target>    # Set target chip (once per project)
idf.py build                 # Compile
idf.py -p <PORT> -b <BAUD> flash  # Flash to device (optional)
```

### Common commands
- `idf.py set-target <target>` вҖ” Set chip target: esp32, esp32s2, esp32s3, esp32c3, esp32p4
- `idf.py menuconfig` вҖ” Configure project settings (**must run in a new terminal window**)
- `idf.py build` вҖ” Build the project
- `idf.py update-dependencies` вҖ” Update project component dependencies
- `idf.py partition-table` вҖ” Build partition table and print partition entries
- `idf.py partition-table-flash` вҖ” Flash partition table to device
- `idf.py storage-flash` вҖ” Flash storage filesystem partition
- `idf.py size` вҖ” Show firmware size information
- `idf.py -p <PORT> -b <BAUD> flash` вҖ” Flash firmware (default baud: 1152000)
- `idf.py -p <PORT> monitor` вҖ” Open serial monitor
- `idf.py -p <PORT> -b <BAUD> monitor` вҖ” Open serial monitor with specific baud (e.g. 1152000)
- `idf.py -p <PORT> -b <BAUD> flash monitor` вҖ” Flash then monitor
- `bash {baseDir}/scripts/monitor_auto_attach.sh --project <PROJECT_DIR> --idf <IDF_DIR> --port <PORT> --baud <BAUD>` вҖ” Auto attach serial (usbipd) and retry monitor on open failure
- `bash {baseDir}/scripts/flash_with_progress.sh --project <PROJECT_DIR> --idf <IDF_DIR> --port <PORT> --mode <MODE>` вҖ” Flash with real-time progress output (supports auto usbipd attach retry, error summary, and second retry)

#### flash_with_progress дҪҝз”ЁзӨәдҫӢ

```bash
# жҷ®йҖҡзғ§еҪ•ж•ҙдёӘеӣәд»¶пјҲеёҰиҝӣеәҰпјү
bash {baseDir}/scripts/flash_with_progress.sh \
  --project <PROJECT_DIR> \
  --idf <IDF_DIR> \
  --port <PORT> \
  --mode flash

# еҠ еҜҶзғ§еҪ• appпјҲеёҰиҝӣеәҰпјү
bash {baseDir}/scripts/flash_with_progress.sh \
  --pr

[Workflow decision tree] - If the user has **no project yet** вҶ’ create from example/template; confirm target chip and IDF version.
- If **build fails** вҶ’ collect the *first* error lines; identify missing deps/toolchain/cmake/python packages; confirm IDF env.
- If **flash fails** вҶ’ confirm PORT permissions/WSL USB passthrough, baud rate, boot mode, correct chip target.
- If **monitor is gibberish** вҶ’ wrong baud (monitor uses app baud), wrong serial adapter settings, or wrong console encoding.
- If **boot loop / panic** вҶ’ request panic backtrace; decode with `addr2line` (or `idf.py monitor` built-in) and check partition/sdkconfig.
- If **`[Errno 11] Could not exclusively lock port <PORT>`** вҶ’ serial port is occupied by another process; force release it with:
  ```bash
  sudo fuser -k <PORT>
  ```

[What to ask the user for (minimal)] 1) Chip target: e.g. `esp32`, `esp32s2`, `esp32s3`, `esp32c3`, `esp32p4`.
2) ESP-IDF version + how itвҖҷs installed/activated (IDF Tools installer vs git clone; `IDF_PATH` / `ESPIDF_ROOT`).
3) Project path + whether itвҖҷs an ESP-IDF project (has `CMakeLists.txt`, `main/`, `sdkconfig`).
4) Serial port path: use `<PORT>` (e.g. `/dev/ttyUSB0`, `/dev/ttyACM*`, or WSL mapped `/dev/ttyS*`).
5) Exact failing command + the *first* error block in output.

[дёІеҸЈи®ҫеӨҮиҺ·еҸ–пјҲWindows + WSL2пјү] е…ҲеңЁ Windows PowerShell жүҫеҲ°дёІеҸЈеҸ·пјҢеҶҚеңЁ WSL2 йҮҢжҳ е°„ USB и®ҫеӨҮгҖӮ

### е®үиЈ… usbipd-winпјҲWindowsпјҢжҺЁиҚҗпјү

еңЁ **з®ЎзҗҶе‘ҳжЁЎејҸ** PowerShell дёӯжү§иЎҢпјҡ

```powershell
winget install dorssel.usbipd-win
```

з„¶еҗҺжҹҘзңӢе№¶жҳ е°„и®ҫеӨҮпјҡ

```powershell
# Windows PowerShellпјҡжҹҘзңӢ USB и®ҫеӨҮдёҺ BUSID
usbipd list

# е°Ҷи®ҫеӨҮжҢӮиҪҪеҲ° WSLпјҲжҜҸж¬ЎйҮҚж–°жҸ’жӢ” USB еҗҺйғҪиҰҒйҮҚж–°жү§иЎҢпјү
usbipd attach --wsl --busid=<BUSID>
```

### дёІеҸЈиҮӘеҠЁжҳ е°„пјҲжҺЁиҚҗпјү

еҸҜзӣҙжҺҘдҪҝз”Ёи„ҡжң¬иҮӘеҠЁйҖүжӢ©е№¶жҢӮиҪҪдёІеҸЈи®ҫеӨҮпјҲдјҳе…ҲйҖүжӢ© Connected еҢәеҹҹдёӯзҡ„ serial и®ҫеӨҮпјҢдё”дјҳе…Ҳ STATE=Sharedпјүпјҡ

```bash
bash {baseDir}/scripts/usbipd_attach_serial.sh
```

еёёз”ЁеҸӮж•°пјҡ

```bash
# жҢҮе®ҡ BUSID
bash {baseDir}/scripts/usbipd_attach_serial.sh --busid <BUSID>

# жҢҮе®ҡеҸ‘иЎҢзүҲ
bash {baseDir}/scripts/usbipd_attach_serial.sh --distro <DISTRO>

# жҢүе…ій”®иҜҚзӯӣйҖүи®ҫеӨҮпјҲеҰӮ ESP32 / COMxx / CP210xпјү
bash {baseDir}/scripts/usbipd_attach_serial.sh --keyword "ESP32"

# д»…йў„и§ҲпјҢдёҚжү§иЎҢ
bash {baseDir}/scripts/usbipd_attach_serial.sh --dry-run
```

иҜҙжҳҺпјҡ
- жҜҸж¬Ў USB и®ҫеӨҮйҮҚж–°жҸ’жӢ”еҗҺпјҢ`BUSID` еҸҜиғҪеҸҳеҢ–пјҢйңҖиҰҒйҮҚж–° `usbipd list` жҲ–йҮҚж–°иҝҗиЎҢиҮӘеҠЁжҳ е°„и„ҡжң¬гҖӮ
- йҮҚж–°жҸ’жӢ” USB еҗҺпјҢйҖҡеёёйғҪиҰҒеҶҚжү§иЎҢдёҖж¬Ў `usbipd attach --wsl --busid=<BUSID>`пјҲи„ҡжң¬дјҡиҮӘеҠЁжү§иЎҢпјүгҖӮ
- еңЁ WSL2 еҶ…еҸҜз”Ё `ls /dev/ttyUSB* /dev/ttyACM* 2>/dev/null` зЎ®и®ӨдёІеҸЈиҠӮзӮ№гҖӮ

### йҰ–ж¬ЎйңҖиҰҒжүӢеҠЁеҠ иҪҪеҶ…ж ёжЁЎеқ—пјҲWSL 2.3.11+пјү

еңЁиҫғж–°зҡ„ WSL зүҲжң¬дёӯпјҢеҶ…ж ёйҮҮз”ЁжЁЎеқ—еҢ–и®ҫи®ЎпјҢ`vhci_hcd`пјҲиҷҡжӢҹдё»жңәжҺ§еҲ¶еҷЁжҺҘеҸЈпјүй©ұеҠЁеҸҜиғҪдёҚдјҡиҮӘеҠЁеҠ иҪҪгҖӮ

еңЁ WSL з»Ҳз«Ҝжү§иЎҢпјҡ

```bash
sudo modprobe vhci_hcd
```

[New Feature: Create ESP-IDF Projects] - **Description**: Create a new ESP-IDF project or create a project from an example in the ESP Component Registry.
- **Usage**:
  ```bash
  idf.py create-project <project_name> <project_path>
  idf.py create-project-from-example <example_name> <project_path>
  ```

[Flash Encryption Support (еҠ еҜҶзғ§еҪ•)] зӣҙжҺҘдҪҝз”Ё `idf.py` жү§иЎҢеҠ еҜҶ/йқһеҠ еҜҶеә”з”Ёзғ§еҪ•пјҡ

```bash
# еҠ еҜҶзғ§еҪ•ж•ҙдёӘеӣәд»¶ (bootloader + partition table + app)
idf.py -p <PORT> encrypted-flash

# еҠ еҜҶзғ§еҪ• app еҲҶеҢә
idf.py -p <PORT> encrypted-app-flash

# йқһеҠ еҜҶзғ§еҪ• app еҲҶеҢә
idf.py -p <PORT> app-flash

# йқһеҠ еҜҶзғ§еҪ•ж•ҙдёӘеӣәд»¶ (bootloader + partition table + app)
idf.py -p <PORT> flash
```

[Bundled resources] ### references/
- `references/esp-idf-cli.md` вҖ” concise command patterns + what to paste back when reporting errors.
- `references/idf-py-help.txt` вҖ” captured `idf.py --help` output for quick lookup/search.

To refresh the help text for your installed ESP-IDF version, run:
- `scripts/capture_idf_help.sh`

### assets/
Not used by default.
